Against Selesnya/Mono-Green Landfall: Bring in board wipes, discard, and early spot removal. Against aggro-leaning variants: Add cheap counters, burn, and graveyard hate. Against control/mirror: Bring in card advantage engines and late-game finishers.

midgame_priorities.resource_management: Balance mana between interaction and development. Never overextend into known removal or board wipes. Use cantrips to maintain hand size and dig for answers. Track graveyard usage, as Landfall and Izzet decks often rely on it for value.
midgame_priorities.board_control: Commit threats only when you have card advantage or can answer their answers. Hold back interaction until you see if the opponent is going for a combo or a wide board. Prioritize removing key Landfall enablers or Izzet spell-slingers before they snowball.
midgame_priorities.threat_tracking: Watch for mana bases that indicate combo or control plans. Note when opponents hold up mana for counterspells (typically 2+ mana open on their turn). Track graveyard accumulation for Landfall triggers or flashback/recurse engines. Be alert to token generation or pump spells that signal an aggressive push.

endgame_tactics.finishers: Leverage superior late-game threats like Perigee Beckoner or value engines that out-value the opponent's remaining resources. Win by out-drawing them, removing their remaining answers, and deploying a threat they cannot interact with.
endgame_tactics.when_to_commit: Go all-in when you have established card advantage, have answered or out-valued their interaction, and the board state allows you to push damage or deploy a finisher without fear of immediate wipe. Also commit when the opponent is low on resources and you can close the game in 1-2 turns.
endgame_tactics.when_to_concede: Fold when you are completely out of resources, facing an unbeatable combo that cannot be disrupted, or holding a dead hand to the current board state. Also concede if you are consistently one-for-two'd and lack the card advantage to recover.

threat_responses.against_countermagic: Use split spells, multi-target effects, or discard to remove counterspells before they can be cast. Deploy uncounterable threats or value engines that win even if countered. Force them to use multiple counterspells by threatening multiple lines of play.
threat_responses.against_ramp: Apply early pressure with spot removal and discard to disrupt their mana development. Use board wipes to reset their board when they commit to a wide or high-value board. Prioritize removing their key ramp sources before they can cast their finishers.
threat_responses.against_aggro: Stabilize with cheap counters and spot removal. Dig for card advantage to outlast their burst. Do not overcommit to the board; instead, trade efficiently and win the resource war. Bring in additional removal and discard post-sideboard.
threat_responses.against_control: Apply consistent pressure with mid-game threats and card advantage. Force them to use interaction on your threats rather than their own game plan. Sideboard in hate cards that disrupt their combo or control shell. Win by out-valuing them in the late game.
threat_responses.vs_mirror: Card advantage wins the mirror. Hold up interaction to punish overextensions. Win the resource war by drawing more cards and deploying threats that force unfavorable trades. Sideboard in additional card draw and late-game finishers to outlast them.

sideboard_advice: Post-sideboard, shift from raw control to targeted hate. Against spell-heavy decks, increase counterspells and discard. Against ramp/aggro, prioritize board wipes and early removal. Against control, add card advantage and late-game threats. Always track the opponent's sideboard strategy and adjust your hate cards accordingly. Avoid overloading on one type of hate; maintain a balance between interaction and win conditions.
notes: Common mistakes include overplaying interaction on low-impact threats, failing to track the opponent's graveyard, and mulliganing too aggressively. Always prioritize card advantage in control matchups, and never assume a threat is dead if you have a way to answer their answer. Practice recognizing when to pivot from control to aggressive value based on the board state.

philosophy: Dimir Reanimator seeks to disrupt the opponent's early game with cheap spells and discard, then rapidly fill the graveyard to reanimate a high-impact threat. It wins by delivering sudden, unblockable damage while controlling midrange and spells-based archetypes through hand disruption and graveyard resource denial.

opening_strategy.mulligan_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands (prioritizing fetches/duals for consistent Dimir fixing), at least one cheap disruption spell (Deceit), and a reanimation payoff or graveyard enabler. Mulligan hands with 0-1 lands, only expensive spells, or no interaction/reanimation tools. Keep any hand with Winternight Stories plus a land and a cheap spell, as it provides immediate engine value.
opening_strategy.early_game_plays: Turn 1: Play a land, cast Deceit if the opponent plays a land or casts a non-basic spell, or hold for a better spot. Turn 2: Develop mana, cast a second disruption or begin filling the graveyard with cheap spells/sacrifice outlets. Prioritize untapped lands or fetches to ensure consistent blue-black mana. Use Winternight Stories to dig for reanimation spells or protect your graveyard if the opponent shows early disruption.
opening_strategy.sideboard_plan: Against Izzet Spells/Spellementals/Prowess: Bring in graveyard hate, additional discard, and spot removal. Against Selesnya/Mono-Green Landfall: Bring in board wipes, artifact/enchantment removal, and early interaction. Against control/mirror: Bring in extra reanimation targets, card draw, and protection spells. Remove redundant disruption and keep your mana base flexible for post-sideboard matchups.

midgame_priorities.resource_management: Balance mana for interaction versus reanimation. Do not overcommit to the graveyard without a reanimation payoff in hand. Use hand disruption to protect your reanimation spells and manage your graveyard carefully to avoid giving opponents free targets for their own synergies or graveyard hate.
midgame_priorities.board_control: Hold back reanimation threats until you have protection or the board is clear of sweepers. Use cheap spells to clear early threats or disrupt opponent key plays. Commit when you can follow up with additional value or have answered their removal. Prioritize clearing the board if the opponent is developing a wide or high-toughness strategy.
midgame_priorities.threat_tracking: Watch for sweepers, graveyard hate, and counterspells. Note when opponents tap out for key spells or leave mana open for interaction. Track their graveyard size and board state to anticipate their win conditions. Pay attention to when they discard or draw, as this may signal their game plan or lack of answers to your reanimation threats.

endgame_tactics.finishers: Superior Spider-Man is the primary finisher. Get there by filling the graveyard with cheap spells, then casting reanimation spells when you have mana and protection. Use Winternight Stories to dig for the reanimation spell or to recur Superior Spider-Man if it dies unexpectedly.
endgame_tactics.when_to_commit: Go all-in when you have a reanimation spell in hand, a threat in the graveyard, and enough mana to cast it plus a protection spell or follow-up. Also commit if the opponent has no sweepers or graveyard hate in play, or if they are tapped out for a key spell. Use Deceit to remove a blocker or answer a threat before committing.
endgame_tactics.when_to_concede: Fold if you are down to a single land, have no interaction or reanimation tools, and the opponent has a clear path to victory. Also concede against graveyard hate/sweeper combos if you have no way to recover. Do not bluff reanimation if the opponent has proven answers and you lack the resources to follow up.

threat_responses.against_countermagic: Use cheap spells to force counterspells, then reanimate with uncounterable effects or stack your reanimation spell on top. Use flashback, exile, or instant-speed recursion to bypass counters. Hold up blue mana for counterspells if the opponent is known for heavy disruption, but do not overcommit to interaction at the expense of your reanimation plan.
threat_responses.against_ramp: Apply early pressure with discard and cheap removal. Do not let them stabilize; reanimate threats before they can deploy their mana rocks. Use graveyard hate if they rely on it. Prioritize removing their mana sources and disrupting their early plays to delay their win condition.
threat_responses.against_aggro: Prioritize early disruption and cheap removal. Hold up mana for counterspells or spot removal to protect your reanimation plan. Reanimate a threat once you have stabilized the board. Use Deceit to remove key blockers or answer aggressive threats. Do not overextend into sweepers or board wipes.
threat_responses.against_control: Trade resources efficiently. Use hand disruption to dig through their deck. Reanimate threats when they are tapped out or out of counterspells. Use card draw to out-value them. Force them to use their answers on your cheap spells, then strike with Superior Spider-Man when they are out of resources.
threat_responses.vs_mirror: Win the resource war. Prioritize graveyard hate, discard, and protection spells. Force them to use their reanimation spells inefficiently. Control the board with spot removal and sweepers when necessary. Track their graveyard and hand to anticipate their reanimation threats, and use Deceit to disrupt their setup.

sideboard_advice: Post-sideboard, tailor your interaction to the metagame. Against fast spells/landfall decks, increase graveyard hate and early interaction. Against control/mirror, add more reanimation targets, card draw, and protection. Remove redundant disruption and keep your mana base flexible. Always track opponent sideboards and adjust your play order accordingly. Prioritize keeping hands with lands, interaction, and reanimation tools over raw card count.
notes: Common mistakes include overcommitting to the graveyard without reanimation payoffs, holding up too much mana for interaction, and failing to track opponent graveyard hate. Unique interactions: Winternight Stories likely provides recursion or card advantage; use it to fuel your graveyard or dig for reanimation spells. Superior Spider-Man may have enter-the-battlefield or death triggers; time your reanimation to maximize value. Always respect the opponent's mana and plan your reanimation around their most likely answers. Practice stacking spells to bypass counters and protect your reanimation threats.
